examples.title: Programmed I/O Example
examples.description: An example of programmed I/O in pseudocode.
examples.code:
READ data FROM device
STORE data IN memory
examples.explanation: This pseudocode example illustrates the concept of programmed I/O, where the CPU directly controls the flow of data between devices.

examples.title: Interrupt-driven I/O Example
examples.description: An example of interrupt-driven I/O in C.
examples.explanation: This C example illustrates the concept of interrupt-driven I/O, where the CPU is notified by an interrupt signal when a device is ready for data transfer.

examples.title: DMA Example
examples.description: An example of DMA in Java.
examples.explanation: This Java example illustrates the concept of DMA, where a device directly accesses memory without CPU intervention.
